steps.title: Identify the disconnection between reality and emotion
steps.content: Both “like a culprit” and “expecting a rebuke” express guilt and fear. Yet Caravan has done nothing wrong—he maintains the same reliable routine every day. These phrases reveal an internal emotional state that contradicts his actual situation.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
The phrases depict an unexplained nervous tension, exactly as choice A states.

Caravan's self-interpretation as guilty (culprit, deserving rebuke) clashes with his consistent, thirty-year track record of reliability.

Choices B, C, and D all blame external factors (monotony, unreasonable expectations, genuine failure), but the underlined phrases emphasize only his unjustified internal anxiety.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
